---
title: "Exploration des accidents de la route genevoise en 2024"
output: pdf_document
---
```{r package_setup, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.width = 9,   # increase width
  fig.height = 9,
  out.width = "100%"   # VERY IMPORTANT
)

library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(lubridate)
library(here)
```
```{r data_import, include = FALSE}
# Load the shapefile
accidents <- st_read(here::here('tp2/OTC_ACCIDENTS-SHP/OTC_ACCIDENTS.shp'))
```

```{r data_processing_2024, include = FALSE}
# Load and explore the data structure
str(accidents)
head(accidents, 5)
```

```{r data_processing_2024_filter, include = FALSE}
# Filter for 2024 using the ANNEE column
accidents_2024 <- accidents %>%
  filter(ANNEE == 2024)

# Create a summary by cause
# Calculate: killed, injured (light + serious), simple crashes (no casualties)
accident_summary <- accidents_2024 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(CAUSE) %>%
  summarise(
    severe = sum(TUES, na.rm = TRUE) + sum(BLES_GRA, na.rm = TRUE),
    light_injured = sum(BLES_LEG, na.rm = TRUE),
    simple_crash = sum((TUES == 0 & BLES_LEG == 0 & BLES_GRA == 0), na.rm = TRUE),
    total_accidents = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_accidents)) %>%
  slice(1:10)  # Keep only top 10 causes
```

```{r summary_stats}
# Calculate key statistics
total_deaths <- sum(accidents_2024$TUES, na.rm = TRUE)
total_injured <- sum(accidents_2024$BLES_LEG, na.rm = TRUE) + sum(accidents_2024$BLES_GRA, na.rm = TRUE)
total_accidents <- nrow(accidents_2024)
```

## Principaux résultats - 2024

- **Total des accidents:** `r total_accidents`
- **Total des décès:** `r total_deaths`
- **Total des blessés:** `r total_injured`

```{r plot_accidents_by_cause, fig.height = 6}
# Prepare data for vertical bar plot
plot_data <- accident_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(severe, light_injured, simple_crash),
    names_to = "consequence",
    values_to = "count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    consequence = factor(consequence, levels = c("simple_crash", "light_injured", "severe")),
    # Reorder causes by total accidents
    CAUSE = reorder(CAUSE, total_accidents, FUN = function(x) -x[1])
  )

# Create vertical bar plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = CAUSE, y = count, fill = consequence)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("simple_crash" = "#FFA500", "light_injured" = "#FF6B6B", "severe" = "#8B0000"),
    labels = c("simple_crash" = "Accident simple", "light_injured" = "Blessures légères", "severe" = "Grave/Décès")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Accidents de la route genevoise en 2024 par cause",
    x = "Cause de l'accident",
    y = "Nombre d'accidents",
    fill = "Conséquence"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r map_accidents_2024}
# Create map of accident locations with severity coloring
# Add severity category to accidents_2024
accidents_2024_severity <- accidents_2024 %>%
  mutate(
    severity = case_when(
      TUES > 0 | BLES_GRA > 0 ~ "Grave",
      BLES_LEG > 0 ~ "Léger",
      TRUE ~ "Simple"
    ),
    severity = factor(severity, levels = c("Simple", "Léger", "Grave"))
  )

# Filter out any geometry issues
accidents_2024_valid <- accidents_2024_severity[!is.na(st_geometry(accidents_2024_severity)), ]

# Create the map
ggplot(accidents_2024_valid) +
  geom_sf(aes(color = severity), size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Simple" = "#FFA500", "Léger" = "#FF6B6B", "Grave" = "#8B0000"),
    labels = c("Simple" = "Accident simple", "Léger" = "Blessures légères", "Grave" = "Grave/Décès")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Localisation des accidents de la route genevoise en 2024",
    subtitle = "Coordonnées CH1903+ / LV95",
    color = "Gravité"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  coord_sf()
```
